Category: Media & Entertainment: Publishing, Film, Advertising & Communications, Gaming
Who is this for: Content managers, digital marketing specialists, social media managers, product managers, UX designers, brand managers, communications specialists, editorial staff, advertising executives
Team or Dept this is for: Marketing, Communications, Product Development, Editorial, Brand Management, Digital Media, Customer Experience
Description: Bias, echo chambers, and audience manipulation.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how streaming, social media, or online publishing platforms work is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify how AI-driven recommendation systems operate across publishing, film, advertising, and gaming, and explain the key concepts of algorithmic bias, echo chambers, and audience manipulation. You will also apply practical frameworks to recognise and critically evaluate the ethical implications of content recommendation technologies in real-world media and entertainment contexts.
